content: The language learning sense of "reflexion" as iterative vocabulary and critical thinking practice can be framed as a human analogue to improve LLM self-reflection mechanisms, where LLMs should practice repeated "vocabulary-level" debugging of their reasoning steps rather than only reflecting on high-level outputs. Just as language learners refine their understanding of a word's nuance through repeated contextual reflection, LLMs can fine-tune their logical primitives through incremental self-reflection at each step of problem-solving.
